heading: Professional science master’s degree programs
label: 1862o–13 Professional science master’s degree programs

children.heading: Development
children.label: 1 Development
children.text: The Director shall establish a clearinghouse, in collaboration with 4-year institutions of higher education (including applicable graduate schools and academic departments), and industries and Federal agencies that employ science-trained personnel, to share program elements used in successful professional science master’s degree programs and other advanced degree programs related to science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: The Director shall make the clearinghouse of program elements developed under paragraph (1) available to institutions of higher education that are developing professional science master’s degree programs.

children.heading: Programs authorized
children.label: 1 Programs authorized
children.text: The Director shall award grants to 4-year institutions of higher education to facilitate the institutions’ creation or improvement of professional science master’s degree programs that may include linkages between institutions of higher education and industries that employ science-trained personnel, with an emphasis on practical training and preparation for the workforce in high-need fields.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the professional science master’s degree program that the institution of higher education will implement;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of how the professional science master’s degree program at the institution of higher education will produce individuals for the workforce in high-need fields;

children.label: C
children.text: the amount of funding from non-Federal sources, including from private industries, that the institution of higher education shall use to support the professional science master’s degree program; and

children.label: D
children.text: an assurance that the institution of higher education shall encourage students in the professional science master’s degree program to apply for all forms of Federal assistance available to such students, including applicable graduate fellowships and student financial assistance under titles IV and VII of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq., 1133 et seq.).20 U.S.C. 1070

children.label: A
children.text: located in States with low percentages of citizens with graduate or professional degrees, as determined by the Bureau of the Census, that demonstrate success in meeting the unique needs of the corporate, non-profit, and government communities in the State, as evidenced by providing internships for professional science master’s degree students or similar partnership arrangements; or

children.label: B
children.text: that secure more than two-thirds of the funding for such professional science master’s degree programs from sources other than the Federal Government.

children.heading: Number of grants; time period of grants
children.label: 4 Number of grants; time period of grants

children.heading: Number of grants
children.label: A Number of grants
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriated funds, the Director shall award grants under paragraph (1) to a maximum of 200 4-year institutions of higher education.

children.heading: Time period of grants
children.label: B Time period of grants
children.text: Grants awarded under this section shall be for one 3-year term. Grants may be renewed only once for a maximum of 2 additional years.

children.heading: Development of performance benchmarks
children.label: A Development of performance benchmarks
children.text: Prior to the start of the grant program, the Director, in collaboration with 4-year institutions of higher education (including applicable graduate schools and academic departments), and industries and Federal agencies that employ science-trained personnel, shall develop performance benchmarks to evaluate the pilot programs assisted by grants under this section.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: B Evaluation
children.text: For each year of the grant period, the Director, in consultation with 4-year institutions of higher education (including applicable graduate schools and academic departments), and industries and Federal agencies that employ science-trained personnel, shall complete an evaluation of each program assisted by grants under this section. Any program that fails to satisfy the performance benchmarks developed under subparagraph (A) shall not be eligible for further funding.

children.label: i
children.text: the results of the evaluation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: recommendations for administrative and legislative action that could optimize the effectiveness of the pilot programs, as the Director determines to be appropriate.
